core_mechanism: continuum (long-wavelength) limit that reduces a discrete nearest-neighbor hopping model on a lattice to an effective linear-dispersion relativistic Dirac wave equation near degeneracy points, where a symmetry-breaking term opens a spectral gap (mass) that reshapes the system's linear-response function
brief_reason: The abstract maps the tight-binding Hamiltonian of a honeycomb lattice with nearest-neighbor hopping onto a 2+1D Dirac-fermion Hamiltonian in the continuum limit and shows that symmetry breaking creating a finite Dirac mass alters the AC (linear-response) conductivity.
